title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied, 40, from Reading, Massachusetts, was sentenced to eight months for trafficking over 100 endangered animal parts.
Bied pleaded guilty to smuggling wildlife parts and violating the Lacey Act, receiving a $75,000 fine and supervised release.
Bied imported parts like orangutan skulls and leopard skins from Cameroon and Indonesia, selling them in the U.S. without permits.

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Adrian Black of Taunton was convicted of first-degree murder for the 2023 fatal stabbing of Milteer Hendricks, 19, in Falmouth.
Prosecutors said Black and a group attacked Hendricks outside an East Falmouth Highway apartment complex; Hendricks was unarmed.
Black, arrested two days after the stabbing, faces life in prison without parole; sentencing is scheduled for Friday.

title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lt. Anthony DeLucia of Massachusetts State Police was suspended with pay after being named in a DA's report.
DeLucia allegedly urged DA's security director to keep quiet about Sgt. Scott Quigley's intoxication during a 2023 crash.
The fatal crash, hidden from the public until 2024, involved Quigley, who was indicted for negligent homicide while intoxicated.

title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker sentenced to 3-5 years for manslaughter in death of foster child.
The infant died in February 2020 from neglect, resulting in treatable conditions like strep infection and bronchopneumonia.
Barlow-Tucker will serve her sentence first, followed by Tucker, to maintain care for their four other children.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, sentenced to life without parole for poisoning boyfriend Leroy Fowler with antifreeze in 2022.
Church was convicted of first-degree murder for spiking Fowler's Powerade with ethylene glycol, a toxic antifreeze chemical.
Prosecutors labeled the couple's relationship 'toxic and dysfunctional'; Church plans to appeal her conviction.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox suffer a 1-5 start to the 2026 season, their worst since 2019, following a series sweep by the Astros.
Boston's pitching staff ranks low in MLB with a 5.22 ERA and struggles with high home runs per nine innings.
Key offensive players like Roman Anthony and Trevor Story struggle, contributing to a team batting average of .208.

title: Former Patriots CB Stephon Gilmore retires from NFL
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Stephon Gilmore, 35, announces retirement from the NFL after a 13-season career via Instagram.
Gilmore played for six NFL teams, winning a Super Bowl and Defensive Player of the Year in New England.
He finished with 617 tackles, 149 pass deflections, and 32 interceptions, including 2 postseason interceptions.

title: Oracle layoffs begin globally; impact on Mass. workforce unclear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Oracle begins global layoffs; unclear if Massachusetts offices in Burlington and Nashua are affected.
Analysts estimate up to 30,000 job cuts, about 18% of Oracle's 162,000 global workforce.
Layoffs follow Oracle's $2.1 billion restructuring plan and industry-wide job cuts in tech sector.

title: Ex-Patriots captain Kyle Dugger reportedly finds new AFC home
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kyle Dugger signs a one-year deal with the Cincinnati Bengals, his third AFC team in three years.
Dugger, a former Patriots captain, was previously traded to the Steelers where he played nine games last season.
During his peak season in 2023 with the Patriots, Dugger recorded 109 tackles and seven pass breakups.

title: Mass. family flies home from vacation to find truck stolen from Logan Airport garage
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brendon Byrnes and his family found their truck stolen from Logan Airport's central parking upon returning from vacation.
The theft mirrored a similar incident weeks earlier in the same garage; both trucks ended up in New Jersey.
State Police are investigating the thefts, noting similarities but have made no arrests yet.
